step0.title: Friends & Servers
step0.content: Tap the ☰ button to open the navigation drawer. Server icons are on the left and your friends list is on the right.

step1.title: Navigation Tabs
step1.content: Use these tabs to manage friend requests, server invitations, or add new friends.

step0.title: Channels & Profile
step0.content: The channel list appears on the right and your profile is pinned at the bottom.

step0.title: Friends List
step0.content: Here you can see your online and offline friends. Search by name or start a conversation.

step1.title: Server List
step1.content: Servers are workspaces where communities live. Click one to explore its channels.

step2.title: Friend Requests & Invites
step2.content: Use these tabs to handle incoming friend requests, server invitations, and to add new friends.

step0.title: Server Channels
step0.content: Here are the text and voice channels for this server. The menu (⌄) next to the name lets you manage the server.

step1.title: Send a Message
step1.content: Try typing something here! Messages are delivered in real time to all channel members.

step2.title: Your Profile
step2.content: Here you can see your status, switch language, or adjust your account.

welcome.author: SystemBot
welcome.body:
Welcome DemoUser 👋
This is a demo environment.
Feel free to explore the server and send messages!

welcome.msg0: Welcome to the demo workspace. This server explains the project and gives the account some believable activity on first load.
welcome.msg1: The goal is simple: when someone enters demo mode, they should immediately understand the product without creating data themselves.
welcome.msg2: Exactly. Empty chat apps feel broken, so the seed should show friends, joined servers, active channels, and natural conversation history.

projectContext.msg0: This project is a real-time chat platform with friendships, servers, channels, and live messaging.
projectContext.msg1: Demo mode is for product walkthroughs, portfolio reviews, and testing the UI without manual setup.
projectContext.msg2: It is also useful for QA because every run starts from a known dataset and the interface stays easy to validate.
projectContext.msg3: If someone asks what to look at first, point them to the friends list, the server switcher, and message history across both servers.

feedback.msg0: Future improvement: add a few pending notifications so the dashboard feels even more alive.
feedback.msg1: Agreed, but first I want the core seed to be deterministic and easy to rerun during development.

aiChatbot.msg0: Welcome to the AI assistant channel! 🤖 Here you can ask the bot anything about the project: its architecture, technologies, features, how it works, and much more. Just type your question and the bot will answer.
aiChatbot.msg1: For example, you can ask: What is this app? What technologies does it use? How does the authentication system work? How was real-time chat implemented?
aiChatbot.name: AI Assistant

seed.seedNote: This is a demo environment. Explore freely!
